canonical_definition: A multilingual question answering benchmark based on Wikipedia, used here for reading comprehension and adapted for open-domain QA by removing context passages.
papers: V-SEAM: Visual Semantic Editing and Attention Modulating for Causal Interpretability of Vision-Language Models

definitions.text: A multilingual question answering benchmark based on Wikipedia, used here for reading comprehension and adapted for open-domain QA by removing context passages.
definitions.paper: V-SEAM: Visual Semantic Editing and Attention Modulating for Causal Interpretability of Vision-Language Models

relationships.evidence: For example, multilingual datasets like TyDi QA (Clark et al., 2020), which includes only Swahili from Africa, AfriQA (Ogundepo et al., 2023), NaijaRC (Aremu et al., 2024), and AfriMed-QA (Nimo et al., 2025). (Section 4.3)

relationships.paper: MoS: Unleashing Parameter Efficiency of Low-Rank Adaptation with Mixture of Shards

iclr25: MoS: Unleashing Parameter Efficiency of Low-Rank Adaptation with Mixture of Shards

relationships.paper: V-SEAM: Visual Semantic Editing and Attention Modulating for Causal Interpretability of Vision-Language Models
relationships.evidence: We adopt the Indonesian set from TyDi QA (Clark et al., 2020) for reading comprehension, which is based on Indonesian Wikipedia with human-written questions. (Section 3.3)
